candidates.text: the localhost link is the most relatable part. everyones first claude code project lives on localhost for way too long because deploying feels like a whole separate skill. once you get past that gap between it works on my machine and its actually live... the whole game changes. next step: ask claude code to deploy it to netlify or vercel for you. one prompt and its done

horrible advice, Claude Code is expensive, slow and will hallucinate, most of these LLMs also think the year is 2024 which is a nightmare while handling infra  dependencies, you are better off using something Claude Code knows well (next.js) and one click deploy on vercel

i accidentally built another app. wasn't planned. i just had a problem and started building

after building my 6th app using ai as a non-technical person, here are a few things that i wish someone told me earlier:

1. you probably haven't planned enough. even when you think you have
2. question the AI on everything. i didn't and it broke my app for 2 days. my fault
3. stop coding on main. use branches. i learned this the hard way
4. get code rabbit or something like it. claude code is great but it still ships bugs
5. set up monitoring from day one. i had no idea my app was going down until users told me

made a video walking through all of it
